item.title: 30 June checklist for SMSFs – planning around the unknown
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
All dates are unique but this 30 June 2021 is unique for so many reasons beyond the calendar reference!  This 30 June represents a transition from the known to the unknown, or more appropriately the uncertain.  This uncertainty is largely based on a number of Federal Budget announcements that make 2021/22 a potential holding pattern for SMSFs but also because we had great expectations for 2021 that were never completely delivered upon. This webinar will look at the unique set of circumstances created by contribution cap and transfer balance cap indexation, the timing of changes to the bring-forward contributions rules, the late announcement of reduced minimum pensions as well as the impact the Federal Budget announcements have on strategic planning for SMSFs.  We will also consider the impact that the removal of other COVID-19 relief measures will have on funds in 2021/22 and beyond.  Being prepared for 30 June 2021 is one thing, being prepared for what lies beyond that date is another. Learning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar attendees will:
  • Contemplate whether Budget announcements can cloud better judgement
  • Be able to identify various contribution and pension strategies for consideration
  • Distinguish between bring-forward, carry-forward and deferred allocation with reference to contributions
  • Have the tools to communicate with their clients beyond 30 June
Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 22 June 2021 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Valuations and SMSF Checklist for Year End Reporting (Advisers)
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
As 30 June approaches there are a number of key issues to be reminded of. This guide will provide an overview and link to a number of those key issues and will take an in depth look at valuation requirements.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Valuations for Year End Reporting (Trustees)
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
It is a legal requirement that all SMSFs record and report all assets at market value.  Failure to report assets at market value or misrepresenting the value of assets can have compliance consequences for SMSF trustees.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Looking under the hood of SMSF benefit payments
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
As an industry we have placed a significant emphasis on recent indexation from the view point of contribution opportunities and pension commencements. Whilst not overlooked or undervalued, benefit payments from an SMSF have been subject to annual indexation since the dawn of time (SMSF time), and they play an important role not only at retirement or death but also throughout other stages of life. This webinar will look at the types of benefit payments made regularly and not so regularly from an SMSF and the different taxation and reporting obligations associated with each. Each payment type will be considered in light of varying conditions of release that members can avail themselves to and the associated obligations that trustees must satisfy to mee their regulatory requirements. Without putting too great an emphasis on pensions versus lump sums, we will also consider appropriate strategic considerations when a member is incapacitated or passes away. Learning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar attendees will:
  • Appreciate the nuances of permanent incapacity versus disability superannuation benefits including modifying lump sum benefits
  • Be able to articulate the pros and cons of taking a superannuation death benefit as a lump sum over a pension
  • Determine the appropriateness of terminal illness benefits
  • Understand the reporting and taxation obligations of paying benefits other than a pension from an SMSF
Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 27 July 2021 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Non-arm’s length expenses – changing old habits
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Over time tax law has evolved to disincentives certain income from being diverted into SMSFs or to discourage trustees from engaging in certain practices to enhance their superannuation balance and reduce their tax liability. Having certain income treated as non-arm’s length income (NALI) and being taxed at the highest marginal tax rate, is intended to be the disincentive in the otherwise concessionally taxed super environment. To broaden the scope of NALI, the Government have introduced the concept of non-arm’s length expenditure (NALE) which requires a fund to ensure that not only is all income derived on a commercial basis but that all expenses associated with fund income are commercial. These new NALE provisions apply from 1 July 2018.

To read this bulletin in its entirety download the PDF version here.

I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom","_inline_size":75.001000000000005},"elements":[{"id":"724f338","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Over time tax law has evolved to disincentives certain income from being diverted into SMSFs or to discourage trustees from engaging in certain practices to enhance their superannuation balance and reduce their tax liability. Having certain income treated as non-arm\u2019s length income (NALI) and being taxed at the highest marginal tax rate, is intended to be the disincentive in the otherwise concessionally taxed super environment. To broaden the scope of NALI, the Government have introduced the concept of non-arm\u2019s length expenditure (NALE) which requires a fund to ensure that not only is all income derived on a commercial basis but that all expenses associated with fund income are commercial. These new NALE provisions apply from 1 July 2018.<\/p>

item.title: SMSFs and the Age Pension
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Subject to satisfying eligibility requirements, self-funded retirees may qualify for Centrelink benefits such as the Age Pension or the Commonwealth Seniors Health Card (CSHC). This fact sheet will run through the key issues that determine eligibility for the Age Pension. We will look specifically at how SMSFs are taken into account for the relevant tests to ensure SMSF members are aware of the opportunities available to them. The key rates and thresholds that apply to the Age Pension are correct at the time of writing.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Six Member SMSFs – the good, the bad and the indifferent
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
From 1 July 2021 SMSFs can have up to 6 members in a fund, an increase from the previous limit of 4.  Anecdotally this has generated far more interest than many people within the industry thought it would which means there is also a thirst for information about the potential ramifications, both good and bad, of increasing fund membership beyond the traditional average of 2. This webinar will contemplate the various pros and cons of adding more members to an SMSF regardless of whether a fund is trying to max out at 6.  Issues to be discussed will include, but not be limited to investment decisions, stapled super, fund fees, segregation, fund control and dealing with disasters.  We will also delve into the impact changing from 4 to 6 will have on other provisions of Superannuation law. Learning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • The impact this change has on various SIS compliance requirements
  • Trust Deed and decision making powers of the trustees
  • The key factors that should influence the decision to add more members to an SMSF
  Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 24 August 2021 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Personal contributions seem so easy until they’re not
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Four years after the rules were expanded to allow all eligible contributors to make personal deductible contributions, rather than those who satisfied the substantially self employed requirements, further changes have been implemented that reduce the penalty for getting it wrong and over contributing.  However, there are still issues associated with the timing of contributions particularly with reference to the ability to provide a notice of intention to claim a deduction comparative to when benefits are drawn from a fund. In this webinar we will review the rules around making personal contributions and highlight the current parameters around excess concessional and non-concessional contributions.  Further we will look at the timing issues that individuals must contemplate when their superannuation world starts to blend contributions with the payment of benefits.  Whilst getting it wrong is not as financially devastating as it once was, there is still so much to contemplate to ensure the outcome of contributing maximises the benefits that individuals expect to receive. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • The requirements to lodge a notice of intention to claim a personal deduction
  • The personal tax impact of making excess contributions, both concessional and non-concessional
  • How benefit payments can impact personal deductions
Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 28 September Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Hardline non-arm’s length expenses approach for SMSFs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The ATO have released Law Companion Ruling LCR 2021/2 which is the finalised version of their draft 2019 ruling “non-arm’s length income – expenditure incurred under an non-arm’s length arrangement”.  It’s fair to say the ruling has the capacity to impact many SMSFs if they don’t change existing practices around fees and asset transfers, as well as other fund expenses, and so it’s critical that SMSF professionals have a clear picture of how the ATO interpret the law so they can advise clients accordingly.  In addition, the ATO have released a draft of their amended contribution ruling that complements the LCR so they have consistency across their publications. This webinar will contemplate the final ruling and the key impact points for SMSFs with an emphasis on identifying the capacity under which certain activities are undertaken, the extent trustees will need to go to establish commerciality and the future of fee discounting.  We will also delve into the impact the ruling will have on how trustees approach asset transfers under the related party acquisition exception rules.  To round things out we will also highlight how non-arm’s length expenses will impact either ordinary or statutory income or both subject to the nature of the expense, and whether there is any light at the end of the tunnel for a fund that inadvertently does the wrong thing. Learning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • Fund members acting in their individual or trustee capacity
  • The impact of NALE on in specie asset transfers
  • How NALE impacts ordinary and statutory income
  • Remedies to ensure non-arm’s length expenses don’t impact a fund
Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 26 October Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSFs and separating members – how relationship breakdowns impact SMSFs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
From 1 April 2022 parties to property proceedings will be able to apply to the family law court registry to obtain information from the ATO about the superannuation information of a former spouse.  This will then allow them to request the necessary information from any relevant super fund to assist in the asset splitting process.  Whilst you could argue that this is less likely to be necessary for SMSFs, as there is a greater likelihood that the spouse is aware of the funds existence, it does highlight that the superannuation splitting process can be quite a complex and onerous one for the parties involved.  The question is, does it need to be? In this webinar we will review the rules around splitting superannuation when there is a relationship breakdown and identify the opportunities that exist within the SMSF space with reference to asset transfers, both from an acquisition and capital gains tax point of view.  Further, we will investigate the difference between making a superannuation agreement or obtaining court orders and what the impact will be to the parties involved. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • The requirements prepare a superannuation agreement both before and at the time of separation
  • The capital gains tax exemptions that apply when assets are split
  • The exceptions to the acquisition of an asset from related party rules as they apply to separation.
  • The process and supporting documentation necessary to effect a payment split
Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 28 November 2021 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSF end game – winding up is the final strategy
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
2020/21 saw the SMSF market record its strongest establishment numbers in over 3 years, with over 25,000 SMSFs established.  2018 was the last time that many funds were established but in that year roughly the same number of funds wound up. The reality is that every fund that is established will be wound up at some stage.  Whilst the establishment process is relatively straight forward, winding up an SMSF comes with many challenges and they can often be misunderstood or overlooked until the last minute and that can result in delays in the process, often dragging a fund into a new financial year. In this webinar we will look at the triggers that prompt members/trustees to contemplate closing their SMSF and work through the practicalities of the wind up process.  We will also consider whether winding up is the best options in all circumstances or whether there are alternative actions that can be taken subject to the reason behind the decision.  In the end winding up is inevitable, it’s just whether all strategies have been explored before making that final decision because once it’s made there is no turning back. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • The reasons why an SMSF might be wound up
  • The alternatives to winding up an SMSF
  • The must do processes that are required prior to a fund being wound up
•             The reporting and record keeping obligations associated with the decision Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 14 December 2021 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

[{"id":"1470ccec","elType":"section","settings":{"padding":{"unit":"px","top":"40","right":"0","bottom":"40","left":"0","isLinked":false},"pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"5610e8d"}]},"elements":[{"id":"a51e18","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"2111ece"}]},"elements":[{"id":"3613cf9d","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Add Your Heading Text Here","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Roboto","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":34,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"300","__dynamic__":{"title":"[elementor-tag id=\"79a24cc\" name=\"page-title\" settings=\"%7B%7D\"]"},"__globals__":{"title_color":""},"title_color":"#0C468A","pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"4488f9b"}]},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"24eebfd","elType":"section","settings":{"structure":"20","pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"e6d04cf"}]},"elements":[{"id":"2b9e392a","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":50,"_inline_size":66.16,"pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"c7ddadc"}]},"elements":[{"id":"1d1ff057","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

2020\/21 saw the SMSF market record its strongest establishment numbers in over 3 years, with over 25,000 SMSFs established.\u00a0 2018 was the last time that many funds were established but in that year roughly the same number of funds wound up. The reality is that every fund that is established will be wound up at some stage.\u00a0 Whilst the establishment process is relatively straight forward, winding up an SMSF comes with many challenges and they can often be misunderstood or overlooked until the last minute and that can result in delays in the process, often dragging a fund into a new financial year.<\/p>

In this webinar we will look at the triggers that prompt members\/trustees to contemplate closing their SMSF and work through the practicalities of the wind up process.\u00a0 We will also consider whether winding up is the best options in all circumstances or whether there are alternative actions that can be taken subject to the reason behind the decision.\u00a0 In the end winding up is inevitable, it\u2019s just whether all strategies have been explored before making that final decision because once it\u2019s made there is no turning back.<\/p>

Leaning Outcomes:<\/p>

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:<\/p>

  • The reasons why an SMSF might be wound up<\/li>
  • The alternatives to winding up an SMSF<\/li>
  • The must do processes that are required prior to a fund being wound up<\/li><\/ul>

item.title: Death Benefits & SMSFs – planning for the inevitable
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The Sole Purpose Test makes provisions for the payment of death benefits to a member’s beneficiaries both as a core and an ancillary purpose of super.  The core purpose provides for death benefits paid prior to a members retirement whereas the ancillary purpose is the payment of a benefit after a member has retired, where the fund has already fulfilled its core purpose of paying the member a retirement based income stream.  No matter the purpose death is a compulsory cashing requirement, and when cashed must be done in one of two forms, an income stream and or a lump sum withdrawal.  The decision confronting surviving trustees when paying out a death benefit can be complicated by the death of a dominant trustee, insufficient documentation, conflicting documentation, conflicted beneficiaries and in some instances the death of the only trustee! This webinar will work through a death benefit checklist to tick off issues associated with paying the benefit, appointing and removing trustees as well as future proofing the death benefit process as much as possible by pre-planning and documenting trustee and member decisions accordingly, including pension commencements. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: •             Trustee powers when a member dies •             Pros and cons of reversionary pensions •             Taxation implications for both the fund and the beneficiaries •             The timeframes that need to be considered for the payment of death benefits Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 02 February 2022 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSFs acquiring assets from related parties
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Section 66 of the Superannuation Industry (Supervision) Act 1993 prohibits, and then makes exceptions for, the trustees of a fund acquiring assets from a related party.  This section in itself does not appear overly complex when contemplating what you can and cannot acquire, however, when read in conjunction with other sections and broader ATO SMSF rulings the issues start to pile up.  Understanding Section 66 requires a solid understanding of the Sole Purpose Test, the prohibition of lending or providing financial assistance to members and/or relatives, the definition of an in-house asset, the exceptions to the definition of an in-house asset and the definition of a related party.  As you can see, there is a fair bit at stake. This webinar will work through an acquisition checklist to tick off issues associated with acquiring assets from a related party.  We will also contemplate the value of the ATO’s SMSF advice service and how that can assist trustees to do the right thing and will also discuss the ramifications of getting it wrong. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for The link between the acquisition rules and other key SIS regulatory requirements How the ATO SMSF Advice program operates What the ramifications are for getting things wrong The timeframes that need to be considered for the payment of death benefits Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 02 March 2022 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

The link between the acquisition rules and other key SIS regulatory requirements<\/p>

How the ATO SMSF Advice program operates<\/p>

What the ramifications are for getting things wrong<\/p>

The timeframes that need to be considered for the payment of death benefits<\/p>

item.title: Borrowing in an SMSF – LRBAs and other rules
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Section 67 of the Superannuation Industry (Supervision) Act 1993 prohibits, and then makes exceptions for, the trustees of a fund from borrowing or maintaining an existing borrowing. Limited recourse borrowing arrangements (LRBAs) are of course the biggest and often most contentious exception to the borrowing rules but there are other long standing exceptions that can create confusion and can be misapplied, particularly within an SMSF. Whilst often the emphasis is on the original transaction i.e. “the borrowing”, the second part of Section 67 prohibits a fund from “maintaining a borrowing” which in effect is to say if you have borrowed inappropriately or have borrowed appropriately but failed the repayment conditions then you can also breach the section. This webinar will work through the various borrowing exceptions, with a particular focus on the issues that often draw the attention of fund auditors. We will also look at the key structural considerations of LRBAs 10 years on from the ATO releasing SMSFR 2012/1 which dealt with the key concepts of LRBAs and how to apply them. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: • When an SMSF can and cannot borrow and the time and value restrictions • Repairs and maintenance versus improvements with LRBAs • Audit considerations and the SMSF penalty regime for breaching s67 • The considerations when an LRBA comes to an end Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 06 April 2022 Date: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSF Contributions and the work test changes
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Government recently delivered on one of its major 2021/22 Budget announcements, which provides individuals with the capacity to contribute to superannuation up to 28 days following the end of the month they turn 75.  Contributions are still subject to an individual’s total superannuation balance, however, trustees no longer need to be concerned with the work status of an individual to accept a contribution rather the emphasis has shifted to the member being satisfied that have met the work test prior to claiming a personal deduction.  This will be one less consideration for SMSF auditors from the 2022/23 year onwards.  Other Budget measures that were passed, specifically the reduction in age eligibility for Downsizer contributions to 60, mean that the entire contribution strategy goalposts move from 1 July 2022. This webinar will go through how the new work test arrangements will work for those aged 67-75 and consider the strategic implications of extending the contribution timeframe, including how they may result in greater excess contributions.  We will also contemplate how these changes impact other strategies such as making small business CGT contributions, undertaking recontribution strategies, transferring benefits from overseas funds as well as commencing pensions. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: How the timing of the downsizer contribution can impact other contribution strategies Whether bring-forward contributions are relevant beyond 2022 The eligibility for the work test exemption beyond age 67 The impact these changes may have on excess non-concessional contributions Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 10th May 2022 (Tuesday) Time: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSF Pension planning toolkit
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Whether it is legislated or not, the primary purpose of superannuation is to pay a benefit, preferably via a pension, to the members of a fund once they retire or obtain a certain age (65).  Paying a pension can be as simple or as complex as you want to make it within an SMSF, it all comes down to the client’s needs and subsequent strategy.  The key issue is whether their strategy has contemplated all there is to contemplate at both establishment and on an ongoing basis.  With the Government extending the 50% discount on minimum pensions for a further 12 months and new contribution rules from 1 July 2022 expanding the capacity to contribute well into retirement, there is no better time to review the key issues associated with paying a pension from an SMSF. This webinar will work through the strategic advantages and disadvantages of the following concepts attributable to account based pensions, including transition to retirement income streams:

Multiple pensions v commute and repurchase

Lump sum withdrawals v excessive pension payments

Discretionary pensions v reversionary pensions

It will also provide a friendly reminder of the impacts that certain actions taken by members and trustees may have on any social security benefits they may receive. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will understand:

there is no one size fits all model for SMSF pensions

that poor decisions can have taxation and/or social security implications for members and beneficiaries

the importance of pension documentation and investment strategy

the factors that distinguish reversionary from non-reversionary pensions

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 8th June 2022 (Wednesday) Time: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Are your SMSFs audit and future proof?
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
July is a great time to start planning for the events of the new financial year whilst also reconciling all matters related to the prior year in preparedness for a Fund’s annual audit.  With a number of key Covid relief measures ceasing from 30 June 2022, exempt current pension income actuarial requirements being amended for 2021-22, contribution and work test updates applying from 1 July 2022 as well as all the usual fund audit requirements, there is plenty to consider. This webinar will work through a number of key end of year/start of year considerations that should be addressed by funds as required, including but not limited to:

Asset valuations

Claiming personal tax deductions for SMSF contributions

Covid relief measures

ECPI deductions

Lump sum withdrawal elections

Pension commencement notifications

Non-standard fund transactions

Investment strategies

Nomination of beneficiaries

Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

SMSF reporting obligations, both ad-hoc and annual

How timing is everything – pensions, contributions, expenses, income

The long term impact of Covid relief items

Reviewing most key SMSF trustee/member decisions annually

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 6th July 2022 (Wednesday) Time: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Property investments in an SMSF
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Whether it’s interest rates, rental occupancy or market demands, property is always a hot topic.  From an SMSF investment point of view property has always been a popular investment sitting in the top 3 asset classes with listed securities and cash and term deposits. Based on 30 June 2020 ATO statistics, property represented about 20% of all SMSF investment, whether held outright or via a limited recourse borrowing arrangement, but if you then factor in property held via managed investments and unit trusts it would likely rival listed shares as the number one investment class.   Property investments attract a lot of attention, so they require a lot of attention. In this webinar we will identify the various ways property can be held in an SMSF as well as any potential issues associated with the choices made having regard for both commercial and residential property.  We will also contemplate the ongoing administration, taxation and property management issues that need to be considered as well as the inevitable liquidity issues property can present. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: - The key distinctions between investing in property held directly versus investing in an entity that invests in property -Who should be undertaking improvements, maintenance and repairs of a property -The taxation considerations of holding property in an SMSF -Strategies that contemplate property and estate planning Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 10th August 2022 (Wednesday) Time: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

[{"id":"1470ccec","elType":"section","settings":{"padding":{"unit":"px","top":"40","right":"0","bottom":"40","left":"0","isLinked":false},"pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"5610e8d"}]},"elements":[{"id":"a51e18","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"2111ece"}]},"elements":[{"id":"24eebfd","elType":"section","settings":{"structure":"20","pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"e6d04cf"}]},"elements":[{"id":"2b9e392a","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":50,"_inline_size":66.16,"pa_condition_repeater":[{"_id":"c7ddadc"}]},"elements":[{"id":"1d1ff057","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

Whether it\u2019s interest rates, rental occupancy or market demands, property is always a hot topic.\u00a0 From an SMSF investment point of view property has always been a popular investment sitting in the top 3 asset classes with listed securities and cash and term deposits. Based on 30 June 2020 ATO statistics, property represented about 20% of all SMSF investment, whether held outright or via a limited recourse borrowing arrangement, but if you then factor in property held via managed investments and unit trusts it would likely rival listed shares as the number one investment class. \u00a0\u00a0Property investments attract a lot of attention, so they require a lot of attention.<\/p>

\u00a0<\/p>

In this webinar we will identify the various ways property can be held in an SMSF as well as any potential issues associated with the choices made having regard for both commercial and residential property.\u00a0 We will also contemplate the ongoing administration, taxation and property management issues that need to be considered as well as the inevitable liquidity issues property can present.\u00a0<\/p>

\u00a0<\/p>

Leaning Outcomes:<\/p>

\u00a0<\/p>

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:<\/p>

- The key distinctions between investing in property held directly versus investing in an entity that invests in property \u00a0<\/p>

-Who should be undertaking improvements, maintenance and repairs of a property<\/p>

-The taxation considerations of holding property in an SMSF<\/p>

-Strategies that contemplate property and estate planning<\/p>

\u00a0<\/p>

item.title: Unit Trust investments in an SMSF
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Following on from our recent property investment webinar, in our next event we will look at unit trusts.  Unit Trust investments can provide SMSFs with greater access to invest in property, greater opportunity to develop property, greater flexibility around such issues as borrowing, unitisation and estate planning as well as simplified single line reporting.  However, along with opportunity comes risk and the risks associated with getting it wrong can result in greater tax liabilities, greater compliance concerns and greater effort having to unwind structures and ensure accurate accounting. In this webinar we will identify the different compliance and tax outcomes that can occur as a result of different types of unit trust investments that can be held by an SMSF.  We will also identify potential issues we see associated with unit trust reporting, particularly around distributions and valuations.  Finally, we will also contemplate any ongoing management issues that need to be considered as well as the inevitable liquidity issues unit trust investments can present. Leaning Outcomes: - At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: - The key distinctions between investing in related versus non-related unit trusts - How property development influences the tax outcomes of trust investments The importance of paying trust distributions as soon possible - How pre-99 unit trusts and 13.22c trusts can work side by side Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 7th September 2022 (Wednesday) Time: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSFs and Social Security
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
With the spotlight in Australia firmly on inflation and the cost of living we’ve seen a number of announcements and measures introduced that provide some relief to age pension recipients and self-funded retirees. Rising interest rates usually mean rising deeming rates, however, the Government have sought to ease people’s minds by maintaining the existing social security deeming rates until 30 June 2024. That, plus the proposed changes to Commonwealth Seniors Health Card (CSHC) income thresholds, mean a greater portion of SMSF members may find themselves eligible for the CSHC, but equally may have them reviewing pre-2015 pensions to determine whether the existing grandfathering rules remain of value. It’s also an interesting time for those looking to downsize as they near or enter the retirement phase to understand the impact that a house sale may have on their age pension entitlement. In this webinar we will look at the potential impact of changes to the CSHC, the impact that reducing the age of eligibility to the downsizer contribution may have and the benefits a well-executed re-contribution strategy can have on couples with a reasonable age gap between them. Of course, no social security webinar would be complete without an overall assessment of eligibility for the age pension. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: • The existing qualification rules for the age pension • How the asset and income test work with reference to accessing various benefits • The impact of various contribution strategies in a post 1 July 2022 expanded contribution universe • Commonwealth Seniors Health card eligibility and the pre-2015 grandfathering rules Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: FREE

Date: 5th October 2022 (Wednesday) Time: 12:30 PM - 01:30 PM AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program.

Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Holding Property in an SMSF – workshop
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
This interactive property workshop, delivered by Tim Miller, will take attendees through all the key considerations of acquiring, maintaining and disposing of property investments within an SMSF. Based on the most recent ATO statistics, property represented about 20% of all SMSF investment, whether held outright or via a limited recourse borrowing arrangement. If you then factor in property held via managed investments and unit trusts it would likely rival listed shares as the number one investment category for SMSFs. With each area as important as the next, this session will delve into the administrative, taxation and audit issues associated with:
  • The key acquisition considerations
  • The definition of business real property
  • How tenants impact the investment
  • Investment strategy considerations – liquidity & diversity
  • Property expenses – NALE, LRBAs, improvements & the link to contributions
  • Selling property – LRBA considerations, member & death benefits
  • Other considerations, including but not limited to titles and valuations.
Relying on the experiences gained from his 25+ years in the Industry, Tim will use a number of case studies taken from real fund examples, and his creative mind, to ensure this workshop provides an interactive experience for all attendees. Beyond the opportunity to engage with like-minded professionals, attendees will also receive a copy of Tim’s “Guide to holding property in an SMSF”, a comprehensive guide that links the technical to the practical. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this workshop, attendees will have an appreciation for:
  • The key distinctions between investing in property held directly versus investing in an entity that invests in property
  • The requirements around undertaking improvements, maintenance and repairs of a property
  • The taxation considerations of acquisition , holding and disposing of property in an SMSF
  • Strategies that contemplate property and estate planning
SuperGuardian would like you to join us for networking drinks immediately following the workshop. Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: $230

Early Bird Cost: $180

Buy one get one free

To bring an associate along we are inviting you to buy one and get one free. Please enter the promo code: TWOFORONE

ADELAIDE

Location:  Mayfair Hotel Date: 17 November 2022 Time: 12:30 pm - 5:00 pm

Register Now

MELBOURNE

Location:  Grand Hyatt Hotel Date: 24 November 2022 Time: 12:30 pm - 5:00 pm

Register Now

Media Partner

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSFs and Crypto Assets
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
In the 15 months from March 2021 to June 2022 cryptocurrency investments held within SMSFs increased by over $1 billion, this is despite the most significant cryptocurrency investment, Bitcoin, dropping in value by almost 60%.  It’s fair to say there has been a spike in interest in SMSFs investing in cryptocurrency and other crypto assets.  In the 2022/23 Federal Budget the Government confirmed its objective to legislate for income tax purposes that cryptocurrencies are not foreign currency, despite some countries declaring it legal tender, El Salvador being the first.  The consultation period for this legislation occurred during September 2022, the legislation will be retrospective to ensure cryptocurrency transactions dating back to a time prior to El Salvador will all be treated according to the ATO’s historical position. So whilst the investment class has a ways to go before becoming mainstream, greater interest inevitably means greater scrutiny from regulators and fund auditors who rightfully need to validate fund transactions not just from an investment restrictions point of view but also for valuation and holding purposes. During this webinar we will outline steps for ensuring cryptocurrency assets are held correctly and flag any associated record keeping requirements, we will also highlight any key issues that may need to be addressed with reference to the trust deed and investment strategy in order to invest in digital assets.  With a focus on existing SIS investment restrictions we will explore non-fungible tokens (NFTs) and consider their current status as an SMSF investment. Leaning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • Record keeping requirements for SMSFs holding crypto assets
  • Determining whether NFTs are an appropriate investment for an SMSF, both now and in the future
  • Identifying the different ways an SMSF can hold crypto assets
Presenter: Tim Miller Date: Wednesday 9th November 12:30pm – 1:30pm AEST

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSFs in 2022 – a year in review
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
2022 has been a landmark year for SMSFs with both Parliament and the Courts impacting major ticket items such as contribution rules and death benefit nominations.  Add to that the rollout of Director IDs, the continued rollout of Superstream (pun most definitely intended), the end of COVID relief for some but not all measures and uncertainty over measures previously announced but never enacted, the result is that 2022 has been a pretty crazy year. This webinar will take a magnifying glass to all the matters that have impacted our industry in 2022, reflect on the good, bad and indifferent of each of the key matters as well as contemplate what 2023 and beyond might hold.  Amongst the key issues that will be discussed will be:
  • Downsizer contribution eligibility
  • Age pension  & CSHC changes
  • Work test restrictions for deductible contributions
  • Non-lapsing binding nominations
  • COVID and in-house assets
  • Minimum pension reductions
  • Residency issues in a holding pattern
  • Legacy pensions
  • Corporate trustee considerations
To round things out we will also highlight how the ongoing uncertainty around certain non-arm’s length expenses (NALE) should not divert our attention from the very real application of NALE rules as they exist  Learning Outcomes: At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:      •  Contribution eligibility and acceptance rules      •  Administrative considerations for SMSF trustees      •  How various measures impact SMSF retirees      •  The dangers of non-arm’s length dealings in an SMSF Presenter: Tim Miller Date: Wednesday 14th December  Time:12:30pm – 1:30pm AEDT Click here to register

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program. Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Transfer Balance Account Reporting
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Transfer Balance Account Reporting

From 1 July 2017, three inter-related concepts were introduced to Superannuation.

• Transfer Balance Cap (TBC) – This is the maximum amount that each individual can transfer into the retirement phase, and is measured as a lifetime cap. The general transfer balance cap is subject to indexation. Depending on when an individual’s first retirement phase pension was commenced will affect what their personal transfer balance cap is.

• Transfer Balance Account (TBA) – Think of this like a bank account. It is used to monitor how much has been transferred in or out of the retirement phase, and the balance changes by way of credits (starting new pensions) or debits (stopping existing pensions).

• Transfer Balance Account Report (TBAR) – This is the report that is sent to the ATO to inform them of events that ‘use up’ your TBC. Ultimately, these events are transactions in your TBA.

To continue reading, download the PDF by clicking here.

item.title: Guide to Pensions in SMSFs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
When a member meets a condition of release, they become eligible to commence a pension using some or all of their superannuation balance. The pension establishes an income stream for the member, with a minimum payment requirement each financial year.

Prior to the commencement of any income stream, the fund’s trust deed must be reviewed to ensure that it allows the commencement of the type of pension requested by the member. Some older deeds may not provide for recent changes to legislation and may prohibit types of income streams that are otherwise now permissible by legislation. If necessary, trustees can amend the trust deed of the fund to allow for all permitted pension types to be paid from their super fund.

To continue reading, download the PDF by clicking here.

item.title: Guide to Transition to Retirement Pensions in SMSFs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
When a member meets a condition of release, they become eligible to commence a pension using some or all of their superannuation balance. The pension establishes an income stream for the member, with a minimum payment requirement each financial year.

Prior to the commencement of any income stream, the fund’s trust deed must be reviewed to ensure that it allows the commencement of the type of pension requested by the member. Some older deeds may not provide for recent changes to legislation and may prohibit types of income streams that are otherwise now permissible by legislation. If necessary, trustees can amend the trust deed of the fund to allow for all permitted pension types to be paid from their super fund.

There are several conditions of release that can be satisfied to commence an income stream. This guide focuses solely on the condition of release of a member attaining preservation age. This restricted condition of release allows people who have reached preservation age to access a portion of their superannuation benefits without having to retire.

To read this guide in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Holding insurance in an SMSF
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
It is mandatory that SMSF trustees formulate, review regularly and give effect to an investment strategy that has regard to whether the trustees of the fund should hold a contract of insurance that provides insurance cover for one or more members of the fund.  Further, post 1 July 2014, trustees can’t provide an insured benefit in relation to a member unless the insured event is consistent with specific conditions of release such as death.  These words are largely derived from the SIS Regulations that form part of the operating standards that SMSF trustees must abide by.  A breach of the operating standards can result in administrative penalties being imposed on the SMSF trustees in their individual capacity.  Interestingly those penalties have increased from 1 January 2023.

This webinar will consider the pros and cons of holding insurance inside an SMSF.  Particular focus will be given to the taxation implications, both positive and negative, to both the member or their beneficiaries and also the fund when holding insurance linked to the payment of the following benefits following the satisfaction of the relevant condition of release:

  • Temporary incapacity
  • Permanent incapacity
  • Terminal illness
  • Death

No insurance presentation would be complete without considering insurance held prior to 1 July 2014, transferring insurance benefits to an SMSF, insurance in retirement phase, lump sums versus pension and of course identifying other reasons why insurance may be held in a fund.

Learning Outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • When insurance premiums are deductible
  • The key differences between temporary and permanent incapacity insurance and benefits
  • The use of the future liability to pay a benefit deduction
  • Investment based insurance requirements

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday 8th February

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEDT

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Education Manager

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program.

Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Webinar - Death of an SMSF member
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The death of an SMSF member can often catch a fund off guard even when the member’s health is such that their death is expected.  There has been a lot of SMSF media coverage about the requirements for making a binding death benefit nomination, whether a payment is a member benefit or a death benefit, the pros and cons of reversionary pensions and of course highlighting the numerous cases where death benefit disputes become the topic for the next SMSF presentation.

In this webinar we will focus on the administrative issues in contemplation of a member passing away.  What does the Trust Deed say about the appointment and removal of both a member and a trustee, what does that same set of governing rules say about binding nominations and reversionary pensions.  What are the legislative or otherwise timing issues of paying a death benefit, adding/removing members and trustees, reporting for transfer balance account purposes and notifying the ATO and in some instances ASIC of the changes.  How does all of this impact the fund?

Learning Outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • The superannuation legislative considerations when a member dies
  • The key considerations of a Trust Deed for dealing with death
  • The reporting obligations for a fund
  • Best practice SMSF death benefit processes

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday 15th March

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEDT

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.
