item.title: Webinar - Demystifying SMSF documentation
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Documentation….you can’t have an SMSF without it yet its need, creation, signing requirements and retention are still cause for much confusion within the industry.  In our March webinar we highlighted the importance of a number of key documents relating to the death of a member, such as the Trust Deed, the nomination of beneficiaries and pension documents, but they are just the tip of the iceberg.  Whether it is member applications, product disclosure statements, trustee consent and declarations, investment strategies, financial statements or the recording of other events there is always something to be documented.

 

In this webinar we will identify the documents that we are questioned about on a daily basis and from that list we will identify which of those documents are mandated by law, which of those documents can be signed digitally and who should be signing the documents.  Further to that we will consider some trustee best practices to ensure that they keep on top of their documentation requirements and needs and ensure that their documents meet their needs.

 

Leaning Outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • Documentation requirements for SMSF trustees and members
  • Signing requirements for SMSF documentation
  • Record keeping requirements for SMSF documentation

 

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Thursday 27th April

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - SMSFs post the Federal Budget
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Following the delivery of the Federal Budget for 2023/24 on Tuesday 9th May 2023 the SMSF industry will turn its attention to what announcements within the Budget will impact our sector and what previously announced changes need to be given the greatest attention in the shortest timeframes.

 

In this webinar we will discuss any and all items that directly impact SMSFs that are announced in the Federal Budget.  Further we will use this session to address the impact of indexation on a number of thresholds and what that means strategically for your clients, we will also review the definition of an Australian Superannuation Fund as that is a matter that has been around since the dawn of SMSFs and continues to create uncertainty with regards to the management and taxation of an SMSF. 

 

Leaning Outcomes:

 

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • Key Federal Budget announcements as they impact SMSFs  
  • The impact of indexation on numerous superannuation thresholds
  • Considerations of an Australian Superannuation Fund

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday 24th May

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program.

Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: Webinar - SMSF end of year checklist
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
June is not a time for procrastinating when it comes to SMSF end of financial year obligations.  It’s the one time of year when timing is everything and for some it becomes the one time of year when they start looking for solutions for the things they don’t get done by the 30th June cut off.  Coincidently, this year 30 June is a Friday so get everything done early in the week and celebrate the end of financial year without any lingering thoughts of “did I do that?”

 

In this webinar we will focus on the key issues that need to be addressed prior to 30 June each year and in the immediate aftermath.  Amongst the issues that we consider will be:

  • Meeting pension obligations
  • Ensuring all contributions are made
  • Notice of intention requirements and 1 July pension commencements
  • Transfer balance account reporting

 

Leaning Outcomes:

 

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • The ramifications of failing to meet pension requirements
  • The rules about contribution acceptance versus allocation
  • How 1 July pension commencements can impact a fund
  • The importance of obtaining non standard asset valuations as soon as practically possible

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday 14th June

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 23 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications and is the author of one of the subjects of the SMSF Association Graduate Certificate delivered through the University of Adelaide and Kaplan, which has also been adopted as part of the CA ANZ SMSF specialist program.

Tim is an SMSF Association Specialist Adviser.

item.title: SMSF Day 2023
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SuperGuardian is the proud principal partner of Smarter SMSF's upcoming full day technical events.

The SMSF Day 2023 event provides you with 8+ accredited CPD hours, a high quality agenda of SMSF technical content – all delivered in a practical way (incl. case study) to help apply directly to your SMSF clients.

Join Aaron Dunn and Tim Miller as they provide insights into the latest developments in the SMSF space and as they work through interactive case studies and demonstrations of the specialist documents your clients need.

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Aaron Dunn & Tim Miller

Dates:
16 March (Melbourne)
22 March (Adelaide)
23 March (Perth)
4 April (Brisbane)
5 April (Sydney)

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

item.title: 2023-2024 Federal Budget Update
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SG Blog:

2023 – 2024 Federal Budget Update | $3 million super threshold confirmed

As expected, the 2023-2024 Federal Budget has placed a strong emphasis on the cost of living and establishing a stronger, secure economy.

From an SMSF perspective, we were pleased to see there were no unexpected changes likely to significantly impact the sector or superannuation more broadly. As expected, we saw continued provisions for the Government’s proposed $3 million tax threshold for superannuation balances.

The following is a brief summary of the key changes relevant to the SMSF sector.

Better Targeted Superannuation Concessions

The Government will be going ahead with its previously announced measure to reduce the tax concessions available to individuals with a total superannuation balance exceeding $3 million, from 1 July 2025. This follows the release of the Government’s Fact Sheet and Consultation Paper on this proposed measure.

This reform is intended to ensure that superannuation concessions are better targeted and sustainable. It will bring the headline tax rate to 30 per cent, up from 15 per cent, for earnings corresponding to the proportion of an individual’s total superannuation balance that is greater than $3 million.

The Government has indicated that earnings relating to fund assets below the $3 million threshold will continue to be taxed at 15 per cent or zero per cent if held in a retirement phase pension account.

The Budget papers also note that this measure will include earning amounts calculated on defined benefit fund interests.

While the precise details on how ‘earnings’ will be calculated under this measure are yet to be finalised, and the Budget papers were silent on the methodology to be applied, the initially proposed model broadly relies on a person’s total superannuation balance to calculate earnings. Unless this proposed approach is modified, unrealised gains, accounting adjustments, and/or book entries and tax refunds will potentially be subject to this new tax.

Note: While the Budget papers state that this measure will include earning amounts on defined benefit fund interests, the papers made no mention of the previously announced measure to allow SMSFs a two-year amnesty period to convert legacy defined benefit pensions.

Non-arm's length income (NALI)

The Government is proposing to amend the non-arm’s length income (NALI) provisions that apply to certain expenses incurred by superannuation funds.

Specifically relevant to SMSF trustees, the Government is proposing to limit the level of a fund’s income that is potentially taxable as NALI to twice the level of an impacted ‘general’ expense.

Additionally, fund income taxable as NALI will exclude contributions.

Treasury had previously proposed that the maximum amount of income, subject to the highest marginal rate, would be five times the level of the general expenditure breach. So, on face value, this proposal would appear to result in an improved outcome for SMSF trustees.

However, further details are required to determine whether this calculation relies on the value of the general expense itself or the level of the general expenditure breach – calculated as the difference between the amount that would have been charged for the general expense under an arm’s length arrangement and the amount that was actually charged to the fund.

Superannuation Guarantee - Changes to payment frequency

From 1 July 2026, employers will be required to pay their employees’ compulsory SG entitlements on the same day that they pay salary and wages. Currently, employers are only required to pay their employees’ SG on a quarterly basis.

This measure will increase the payment frequency of superannuation to align with the payment of salary and wages, ensuring employees have greater visibility over whether their entitlements have been paid and better enable the ATO to recover unpaid superannuation amounts. The increased frequency of payment will also support better long-term retirement outcomes.

This measure was announced prior to the Federal Budget and will provide individuals and their professional advisers greater certainty on the timing of superannuation contributions. From a contribution planning perspective, this is critically important and is expected to help reduce instances of inadvertent contribution cap breaches.

How can we help?

If you have any questions or would like further clarification regarding any of the above measures outlined in the 2023-24 Federal Budget, please don’t hesitate to contact  SuperGuardian.

This content has been provided by The SMSF Association.

The Government has indicated that earnings relating to fund assets below the $3 million threshold will continue to be taxed at 15 per cent or zero per cent if held in a retirement phase pension account.<\/p>

The Budget papers also note that this measure will include earning amounts calculated on defined benefit fund interests.<\/p>

While the precise details on how \u2018earnings\u2019 will be calculated under this measure are yet to be finalised, and the Budget papers were silent on the methodology to be applied, the initially proposed model broadly relies on a person\u2019s total superannuation balance to calculate earnings. Unless this proposed approach is modified, unrealised gains, accounting adjustments, and\/or book entries and tax refunds will potentially be subject to this new tax.<\/p>

Note:<\/strong> While the Budget papers state that this measure will include earning amounts on defined benefit fund interests, the papers made no mention of the previously announced measure to allow SMSFs a two-year amnesty period to convert legacy defined benefit pensions.<\/p>

Non-arm's length income (NALI)<\/strong><\/span><\/h3>

The Government is proposing to amend the non-arm\u2019s length income (NALI) provisions that apply to certain expenses incurred by superannuation funds.<\/p>

Specifically relevant to SMSF trustees, the Government is proposing to limit the level of a fund\u2019s income that is potentially taxable as NALI to twice the level of an impacted \u2018general\u2019 expense.<\/p>

Additionally, fund income taxable as NALI will exclude contributions.<\/p>

Treasury had previously proposed that the maximum amount of income, subject to the highest marginal rate, would be five times the level of the general expenditure breach. So, on face value, this proposal would appear to result in an improved outcome for SMSF trustees.<\/p>

However, further details are required to determine whether this calculation relies on the value of the general expense itself or the level of the general expenditure breach \u2013 calculated as the difference between the amount that would have been charged for the general expense under an arm\u2019s length arrangement and the amount that was actually charged to the fund.<\/p>

Superannuation Guarantee - Changes to payment frequency<\/strong><\/span><\/h3>

From 1 July 2026, employers will be required to pay their employees\u2019 compulsory SG entitlements on the same day that they pay salary and wages. Currently, employers are only required to pay their employees\u2019 SG on a quarterly basis.<\/p>

This measure will increase the payment frequency of superannuation to align with the payment of salary and wages, ensuring employees have greater visibility over whether their entitlements have been paid and better enable the ATO to recover unpaid superannuation amounts. The increased frequency of payment will also support better long-term retirement outcomes.<\/p>

This measure was announced prior to the Federal Budget and will provide individuals and their professional advisers greater certainty on the timing of superannuation contributions. From a contribution planning perspective, this is critically important and is expected to help reduce instances of inadvertent contribution cap breaches.<\/p>

How can we help?<\/strong><\/span><\/h3>

If you have any questions or would like further clarification regarding any of the above measures outlined in the 2023-24 Federal Budget, please don\u2019t hesitate to contact\u00a0 SuperGuardian.<\/p>

This content has been provided by The SMSF Association.<\/strong><\/em><\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"},{"id":"0c21c58","elType":"widget","settings":{"pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"spacer"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

item.title: Webinar - Pension standards: Pitfalls and Planning Opportunities
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
July marks the start of a new financial year and 2023/24 sees the return to normal minimum pension requirements, something that we haven't seen since 2018/19.  It is also the first month that we start to identify situations where clients haven't met their minimum pension obligations for the previous year.  Failing to pay the minimum pension has consequences but those consequences are different subject to the type of pension.  Similarly there are consequences for paying too much pension. All pensions must meet specific pension standards and failure to meet those standards can impact both the fund and the member.

This webinar explores the income tax and SIS issues where a member fails to meet the pension standards, whether it be a transition to retirement income stream, account based pension or death benefit income stream.  Amongst the issues that we consider will be:

  • When a pension ceases
  • Dealing with shortfalls
  • Failing TRIS requirements
  • Death Benefit pension obligations

 

Leaning Outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • the impact of failing the minimum pension, including loss of tax exemption.
  • how benefit payments are treated, subject to the type of income stream being paid.
  • what concessions may be available for the pension to continue.
  • Ideas to contemplate when a pension shortfall may exist in an income year.

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 18th July

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Accredited CPD Education

SuperGuardian’s education activities are assessed against the SMSF Association’s professional education standards. Being recognised as an SMSF Association Accredited Educator enables us to work closely with the Association in lifting the standards of professional SMSF advice and services through quality SMSF education.

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar – The Latest Intel on LRBAs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
In this upcoming webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF we will be exploring a number of current and emerging issues to give you the latest intel of what’s happening with assets acquired under s.67A & 67B, including:

  • The use of intermediary LRBAs, and their impact on a member’s Total Super Balance (TSB)
  • What needs to be considered with the ATO safe harbours for related party loans in a rising interest rate environment (also where Div7A rules apply)
  • The impact of the recent findings by the Council of Financial Regulators (CFR) into the use of LRBAs by SMSFs
  • Structural considerations for when the loan has been repaid

Tim will use a range of examples throughout the session to illustrate each of these topics further.

Leaning Outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  • The impact of increasing interest rates on safe harbour LRBAs
  • The difference between intermediary and standard LRBAs
  • The difference between intermediary and standard LRBAs
  • The in-house asset exemption and exiting LRBAs

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Thursday 24th August

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - SMSF Contributions Strategies - Great opportunities await those that get them right!
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
1 July 2022 opened up the opportunities for many more superannuation fund members to make contributions to their fund and 1 July 2023 provided even more opportunity via the indexation of the transfer balance cap. Whilst the rules for accepting contributions are less onerous for SMSF trustees, there are now fewer protective measures against making mistakes and some of those mistakes can come at a cost

In this upcoming webinar Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will look into the various contribution strategies available and highlight some of the consequences of getting the contributions rules wrong as well as consider some of the lesser appreciated rules about making contributions.

Some of the items that will be discussed include:

  • Work test changes create potential for more excess NCC
  • Eligibility for various contribution strategies
  • Which contributions require an election and when do they need to be made.
  • How to deal with excess contributions.
The webinar will provide a useful one stop spot for many of your contribution regulatory requirements and Tim will use a range of examples throughout the session to illustrate each of the above topics further. 

Leaning Outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:

  •  Understand and analyse the impacts and outcomes of the revised contribution rules
  • dentify and evaluate the pitfalls of different contribution strategies
  • Analyse how those pitfalls will impact your client’s contribution opportunities and
  • Understand and explain the regulatory and compliance issues that can arise including
    • the reduced opportunities to directly refund erroneous contributions
    • The different outcomes associated with excess contributions
    • the different notice election requirements that apply

 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday 20th September

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - SMSF Expenses Under the Microscope
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Following the release of legislation that deals with the ramifications of SMSFs not paying expenses on an arm's length basis. now is a good opportunity to have a look at the type of expenses an SMSF might pay on an annual or ad hoc basis.  With all the focus on the detrimental outcome of non-arm's length expenses (NALE), it shouldn't be overlooked that there are also tax benefits associated with many SMSF expenses.  In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will put various expenses under the microscope, and explores the various types of deductions available within an SMSF as well as all the associated benefits and risks.  Amongst other items, the webinar will cover:   ·       Operating expenses·       Investment related expenses·       Tax-related expenses·       Legal expenses (incl. deed amendments)·       Statutory fees and levies·       Otherwise deductible expenses, such as insurance premiums·       Future service deductions·       How NALI & ECPI impacts the claiming of certain deductions  Learning outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: ·       the types of expenses an SMSF might be liable for.·       the deductibility of various SMSF expenses·       how NALI and ECPI can impact various SMSF expenses

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Thursday 12th October 2023

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - SMSF Benefit Payments - Tips and Traps
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Recent ATO statistics highlight that more and more SMSF trustees are being disqualified for illegal early access of their superannuation benefits.  Whilst the overwhelming majority of trustees and members do the right thing when it comes to withdrawing super, there are plenty of things to consider to ensure the SMSF and/or the members don't fall foul of the rules. In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will delve into the rules around accessing super and will take a deeper dive into the options that avail themselves to trustees when paying a benefit.  Amongst other items, the webinar will cover:   ·  In-specie benefit payments·  Commutations versus pension payments·  Drawing from accumulation v pension interests·  Member benefits v death benefits·  Taxation of benefit payments·  What happens when it goes wrong  Learning outcomes:

At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: ·  the different types of benefit payments available to SMSFs.·  how the nature of the benefit payment can impact the timing and treatment·  the taxation implications of accessing super both legally and illegally 

Disclaimer: By registering, you consent to receiving communications regarding SuperGuardian’s services, including education, newsletter and general marketing communications.

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Thursday 16th November 2023

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

Recent ATO statistics highlight that more and more SMSF trustees are being disqualified for illegal early access of their superannuation benefits.\u00a0 Whilst the overwhelming majority of trustees and members do the right thing when it comes to withdrawing super, there are plenty of things to consider to ensure the SMSF and\/or the members don't fall foul of the rules.<\/div>
\u00a0<\/div>
In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will delve into the rules around accessing super and will take a deeper dive into the options that avail themselves to trustees when paying a benefit.\u00a0 Amongst other items, the webinar will cover:\u00a0\u00a0<\/div>
\u00a0<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0In-specie benefit payments<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0Commutations versus pension payments<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0Drawing from accumulation v pension interests<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0Member benefits v death benefits<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0Taxation of benefit payments<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0What happens when it goes wrong<\/div>
\u00a0<\/div>
\u00a0<\/div>
Learning outcomes:
<\/b>
At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:<\/div>
\u00a0<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0the different types of benefit payments available to SMSFs.<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0how the nature of the benefit payment can impact the timing and treatment<\/div>
\u00b7\u00a0\u00a0the taxation implications of accessing super both legally and illegally<\/div><\/div>
\u00a0<\/div><\/div>

item.title: Webinar - Holding Business Real Property in an SMSF
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SMSFs have long been promoted for their ability to hold business real property, either directly or indirectly via a company or trust.  Concessions apply to allow an SMSF to acquire from and lease to related parties where property satisfies the definition of business real property.  As such it's important to understand the requirements of meeting the definition of business real property in relation to SMSFs for the purposes of the SIS Act.  

In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will take a detailed look at the ATO's ruling on business real property, SMSF ruling 2009/1, and will explore, amongst other things:

  · the meaning of business real property within the SIS laws, including the two basic conditions that must be satisfied to hold it · the business real property context in respect to the related party acquisition rules · Primary production requirements · Land development, vacant land and when is use of real property in a business · the considerations when property doesn't meet the definition     Learning outcomes:    At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for   · whether property satisfies the definition of business real property or not · the SIS concessions that apply when property is business real property · the ongoing requirements that a fund must maintain to ensure its investment continues to be business real property

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday 13th December 2023

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30 AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

SMSFs have long been promoted for their ability to hold business real property, either directly or indirectly via a company or trust.  Concessions apply to allow an SMSF to acquire from and lease to related parties where property satisfies the definition of business real property.  As such it's important to understand the requirements of meeting the definition of business real property in relation to SMSFs for the purposes of the SIS Act.  

In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will take a detailed look at the ATO's ruling on business real property, SMSF ruling 2009/1, and will explore, amongst other things:

 
· the meaning of business real property within the SIS laws, including the two basic conditions that must be satisfied to hold it
· the business real property context in respect to the related party acquisition rules
· Primary production requirements
· Land development, vacant land and when is use of real property in a business
· the considerations when property doesn't meet the definition
 
 
Learning outcomes: 
 
At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for
 
· whether property satisfies the definition of business real property or not
· the SIS concessions that apply when property is business real property
· the ongoing requirements that a fund must maintain to ensure its investment continues to be business real property

item.title: Webinar - How the $3m Division 296 Tax will impact SMSFs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SMSFs have long been promoted for their ability to hold business real property, either directly or indirectly via a company or trust.  Concessions apply to allow an SMSF to acquire from and lease to related parties where property satisfies the definition of business real property.  As such it's important to understand the requirements of meeting the definition of business real property in relation to SMSFs for the purposes of the SIS Act.      

In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will take a detailed look at the ATO's ruling on business real property, SMSF ruling 2009/1, and will explore, amongst other things:

· the meaning of business real property within the SIS laws, including the two basic conditions that must be satisfied to hold it· the business real property context in respect to the related party acquisition rules· Primary production requirements· Land development, vacant land and when is use of real property in a business· the considerations when property doesn't meet the definition  Learning outcomes:  At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for· whether property satisfies the definition of business real property or not· the SIS concessions that apply when property is business real property· the ongoing requirements that a fund must maintain to ensure its investment continues to be business real property  

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 13th February 

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEDT

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia\u2019s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years\u2019 experience in the Superannuation industry.\u00a0 His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.\u00a0 In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.<\/p>

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.<\/p>","pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"text-editor"}],"isInner":true},{"id":"5265c19b","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":50,"_inline_size":30,"pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[{"id":"35b7514","elType":"widget","settings":{"space":{"unit":"px","size":60,"sizes":[]},"pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"spacer"},{"id":"2f4656c","elType":"widget","settings":{"image":{"url":"https:\/\/superguardian.com.au\/wp-content\/uploads\/2024\/02\/TimMiller-2023-02.jpg","id":15016,"alt":"","source":"library","size":""},"align":"left","pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"image"}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

item.title: Welcome to SuperGuardian…
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
CONGRATULATIONS... 

for solving...

Now that problem is solved, check out our Client Service Guide to find out who we are, what we have to offer and how SuperGuardian can help you.

Client Service Guide

While you are here, don't forget to sign up to our SG Events to stay up to date with the latest news, tips and educational materials, including invitations to our monthly SMSF complimentary webinar where you can earn CPD at each session.

SG Events

item.title: SMSF Day 2024
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SuperGuardian is partnering with Smarter SMSF again this year, to bring you SMSF Day 2024. Come and learn from two of Australia’s most well-known and leading SMSF specialists, Aaron Dunn and Tim Miller. SMSF Day 2024 event, provides you with 8+ accredited CPD hours, a high-quality agenda of SMSF technical content – all delivered in a practical way (incl. case study) to help apply directly to your SMSF clients.  Through our popular case study format, the sessions throughout the day will delve into some of the most topical issues in the SMSF sector today, including the latest on the new Division 296 tax laws, the impact of the new NALE/NALI laws, along with key estate planning issues. Importantly, Aaron and Tim will provide a practical context to many of these topics, giving you the knowledge required to apply directly to your SMSF clients. This full day event will be held in Melbourne, Adelaide, Perth, Brisbane and Sydney and we invite you to join. Presenters: Aaron Dunn & Tim Miller Date: SMSF technical full-day events from 13 March – 27 March

Register Now

item.title: Unused Concessional Contributions Cap Carry Forward
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The standard concessional contribution cap, the limit for concessionally taxed employer and personal deductible contributions. It is subject to indexation, based on Average Weekly Ordinary Times Earnings (AWOTE), which results in future increments of $2,500. From 1 July 2024 the concessional contribution cap increased to $30,000.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Annual Trustee Plan
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
It’s the New Year – time for new resolutions and planning the year ahead. For SMSF trustees, reviewing your fund’s investment strategy and individual investments should be top of the list.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Excess Concessional Contributions
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The concessional contribution cap is $30,000 from 1 July 2024. With the ability to carry forward unused concessional contributions over a 5 year rolling period available in some cases, it can make it more complicated to know what concessional contribution cap applies to an individual. This fact sheet will give a general overview of the current position on what happens when the concessional contributions cap is exceeded.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: Excess Non-Concessional Contributions
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
The non-concessional contributions cap is $120,000 from 1 July 2024, however the ability to contribute to super is dependent on an individual's Total Superannuation Balance and satisfying certain age-based criteria. The ability to bring forward non-concessional contributions over a 3-year period in some cases further complicates matters and when there is uncertainty around what an individual’s non-concessional contribution cap is, then excess non-concessional contributions can arise. This fact sheet will give a general overview of the current position on what happens when the non-concessional contributions cap is exceeded.

To read this fact sheet in its entirety download the PDF version here.

item.title: FAQs for LAB Group
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Who is LAB Group?

LAB Group specializes in RegTech, offering digital workflow solutions to streamline complex customer onboarding and automate compliance processes like AML, KYC, KYB, and PEP/Sanctions screening. They provide a user-friendly, easily integrable API solution, benefiting from insights from over 100 regulated entities worldwide. Their services cater to various sectors, including banking, stockbroking, managed funds, and more, emphasizing operational efficiency and compliance. Their customers include Link Group, Bell Potter, Macquarie, Canaccord Genuity and many others. For more detailed information, you can visit their website at https://labgroup.com.au/

 

How is personal information handled by LAB Group?

LAB Group's Privacy Policy outlines their approach to handling personal information. It details the types of personal information collected, the purposes for collection, how the information is collected, and with whom it may be shared. The policy also provides information on how individuals can access their personal information or lodge a complaint. For more detailed information, you can read their full Privacy Policy on their website at https://labgroup.com.au/privacy-policy/

 

What will it mean for my clients?

Any new clients who you refer to SuperGuardian will engage with SuperGuardian via this online form, where they will be asked to provide information, including:

o The name (or preferred name) of their SMSF
o Personal details for each member (names, addresses etc including Tax File Numbers)
o Identification for each member (Driver's License, Passport etc)
o The name of the Corporate Trustee (if applicable) and Director Identification Numbers for each Director
o Details of their Financial Adviser
o Details of the SMSF's current accountant/administraton

The amount of information they need to provide will be less where the information can be accessed from public registers (for example trustee company details available through ASIC) or where information can be provided by you or a previous accountant or administrator. They will then be asked to agree to SuperGuardian's terms and conditions and consent for us to arrange the necessary compliance checks in order for us to proceed with the engagement.


Can I complete the application process for my clients?
Yes, we have applications available through our website (https://superguardian.com.au/lets-get-started/) which are specifically designed for you to complete on behalf of your clients. Once you commence the form, you will need to provide your details as the 'primary applicant' before proceeding to provide client details. Where you provide all of the required information on behalf of your clients, they will receive an email with a secure link to review the information provided, agree to SuperGuardian's terms and conditions and consent for us to arrange the necessary compliance checks.

 

Can I still submit a paper-based application form?
If your client would prefer to sign a paper form, this is possible through LAB, please just select the 'print, sign and post' option when you commence the online form. Once you have provided the necessary information, you will be able to print the form, which can then be posted or scanned to us once the client has signed.

item.title: Webinar - Does indexation impact your SMSF clients?
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
There are three key indexation factors that impact the SMSF industry and combined they have an effect on contribution strategies and opportunities, pension planning as well as LRBA management so it's fair to suggest that understanding their impact is a critical element to  managing your SMSF clients.

In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will take a look at why we pay such close attention to the consumer price index (CPI), average weekly ordinary times earnings (AWOTE) and the Reserve Bank Indicator lending rates and the impact they have on various superannuation thresholds.  2024 is a significant year as we see age pension age max out at 67, preservation age max out at 60 and some thresholds change whilst others remain the same.  The session will consider the impact of all these changes. 

 Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: ·  how CPI impacts certain thresholds and what that means for 2024/25 ·  how AWOTE impacts certain thresholds and what that means for 2024/25 ·  how the Reserve Bank Indicator lending rates influence the SMSF decision making process ·  the significance of changes to preservation age and age pension age

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 19th March 

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEDT

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

 

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - The SMSF winding up window is always open
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Winding up is not a decision SMSF trustees should be making in June, unless they're giving themselves 12 months to wind up a fund.  There are numerous considerations to ponder prior to making the final decision to wind up because once a fund is wound up there is no going back.   

In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will work through an SMSF wind up checklist to check that all things have been considered and winding up is the right decision.  The session will then delve into the wind up process to ensure all the appropriate steps are taken to make sure the SMSF is wound up without any nasty surprises to come.  Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: ·  the matters that may influence trustees to retain the SMSF ·  the matters that may influence trustees to close the SMSF ·  the necessary steps that need to be taken to wind up an SMSF ·  record keeping obligations once a fund is wound up  

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 16th April

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEDT

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - Don't neglect your SMSF documentation needs
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
If there's a common denominator in most SMSF conflicts that end up in the Court system it's usually the fund/member documentation, or lack thereof.  Whether it's missing documents, documents that are in direct conflict with each other, non-adherence to terms stipulated within certain documents or just meaningless documents, it's an area that needs greater scrutiny and ongoing maintenance. In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will identify key events that require documentation based on legislative requirements as well as those that are predominantly transactional based, the importance of ensuring documents is prepared by appropriately qualified parties, as well as record keeping and signing requirements. Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: · The importance of ensuring a funds governing rules are fit for purpose. · The must have documents for all SMSFs. ·The documents that cause the greatest conflict. · The need to review documents on a regular basis. Presenter: Tim Miller Cost: Free Date: Tuesday 21st May Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEDT

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - Insurance, Transfer Balance Caps & Div 296
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SMSF Trustees are required as part of their investment strategy to consider holding insurance for fund members.  Insurance policies are a form of investment that many hopes will never be realised but when they are it's important to know what impact they may have on fund members and/or their beneficiaries but also what impact they can have on a fund. In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will highlight the pros and cons of holding insurance inside an SMSF for members, for a member's beneficiaries and for the fund itself.  The session will identify how insurance proceeds are treated when they hit a fund, the impact insurance has on the transfer balance cap and contemplate how insurance will interplay with the projected introduction of Division 296 tax. Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: · The types of insurance that can be held inside a fund. ·The taxation benefits and traps of having insurance inside super. ·How holding insurance in particular member interests will be more or possibly less beneficial. ·How insurance is impacted by superannuation policy changes. Presenter: Tim Miller Cost: Free Date: Tuesday 18th June Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEDT

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter. Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - SMSF estate planning considerations
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SMSF trustees have a minimum annual requirement to review the fund's investment strategy and whilst no such regulatory requirement exists for reviewing the fund's succession plan, it is just as critical.  Having an appreciation for what the SMSF trustee structure might look like when a member passes away is just as important and having clear and concise instructions from the members as to how they want their benefits to be paid.

 In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will highlight the key considerations when it comes to estate planning within an SMSF.  The session will consider the trustee requirements with regards to the death of both individual trustees and directors of corporate trustees as well as the member considerations covering who can receive a death benefit, the tax implications for the recipient and ensuring death benefit nominations are appropriately documente Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: ·Who controls the fund when a member dies ·What various nomination options available for an SMSF member ·Member benefits v death benefits in an SMS ·Are beneficiaries superannuation and taxation dependents  

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 23rd July

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEDT

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - The problem dealing with related parties
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
A number of recent Court cases have highlighted that dealing with related parties can be both convenient and yet complex at the same time, as Fund Auditors and indeed the Regulator look beyond the transaction itself to determine whether its purpose and application satisfy the various superannuation investment restrictions.  At their very core, SMSF related party transactions will need to pass more than one regulatory provision and often that number is 3 or more once you factor taxation. In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will use recent case outcomes to highlight the many key investment considerations that must be undertaken every time SMSF trustees contemplate dealing with a related party, including the initial trigger of whether the parties are related or not. Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: ·How the sole purpose test sits behind every decision a trustee makes ·Who is a related party of an SMSF ·The various investment restrictions beyond the sole purpose test that impact related party transactions ·The impact of getting related party transactions wrong  

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 20th August

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

[{"id":"1470ccec","elType":"section","settings":{"padding":{"unit":"px","top":"40","right":"0","bottom":"40","left":"0","isLinked":false},"pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[{"id":"a51e18","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":100,"_inline_size":null,"pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[{"id":"3613cf9d","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Add Your Heading Text Here","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Roboto","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":34,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"300","__dynamic__":{"title":"[elementor-tag id="79a24cc" name="page-title" settings="%7B%7D"]"},"__globals__":{"title_color":""},"title_color":"#0C468A","pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"24eebfd","elType":"section","settings":{"structure":"20","pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[{"id":"2b9e392a","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":50,"_inline_size":66.16,"pa_condition_repeater":[],"premium_tooltip_text":"Hi, I'm a global tooltip.","premium_tooltip_position":"top,bottom"},"elements":[{"id":"1d1ff057","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"
A number of recent Court cases have highlighted that dealing with related parties can be both convenient and yet complex at the same time, as Fund Auditors and indeed the Regulator look beyond the transaction itself to determine whether its purpose and application satisfy the various superannuation investment restrictions.  At their very core, SMSF related party transactions will need to pass more than one regulatory provision and often that number is 3 or more once you factor taxation.
 
In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will use recent case outcomes to highlight the many key investment considerations that must be undertaken every time SMSF trustees contemplate dealing with a related party, including the initial trigger of whether the parties are related or not.
 
Learning Outcomes
 
At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
 
·How the sole purpose test sits behind every decision a trustee makes
 
·Who is a related party of an SMSF
 
·The various investment restrictions beyond the sole purpose test that impact related party transactions
 
·The impact of getting related party transactions wrong

item.title: Webinar - A guide to SMSF investment strategies
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
SMSF trustees have a requirement to regularly review their fund's investment strategy and there is no doubt that this regulatory obligation is coming more and more under the scrutiny of the ATO.  In the past couple of years we have seen the ATO communicating with funds and auditors about market valuations and diversification and there has been a slew of investment related court cases of recent times, leading to the conclusion that investment decisions have to start somewhere, and that place is the investment strategy. 

In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will highlight the key requirements for the creation and maintenance of an SMSF investment strategy as well as identify when a review should occur and how this can be recognised by the trustees.  The session will consider how much detail a strategy needs to contain as well as identify some of the other regulatory quirks that may be suited to the investment strategy. vLearning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • The ATO must haves in an SMSF investment strategy 
  • Building a strategy to meet a fund's objectives 
  • Dealing with issues such as insurance and other non-standard considerations 
  • How to regularly review the strategy and when it should be changed

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Tuesday 17th Sept

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - Asset segregation and ECPI in an SMSF
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
Self-managed super funds are subject to special rules when it comes to being able to segregate assets to support a pension for income tax purposes, however, this doesn't mean an SMSF that doesn't satisfy the rules is unable to segregate assets.  Segregation is not just about tax exemption, although that is a big consideration, it's also about member choice and how investments are used to meet a fund's objectives. In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will work through the actuarial requirements for an SMSF paying a retirement phase pension including when an SMSF can and can't segregate and when segregation is deemed to occur naturally.  Further, Tim will delve into the area of asset segregation for member choice and investment strategy purposes.  Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for:
  • how the disregarded small fund asset rules apply to pension establishment and ongoing pension management
  • Actuarial requirements, including choices, for SMSF trustees
  • segregating assets for SIS purposes rather than tax purposes
  • segregation tips and traps

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Thursday, 17th October

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.

item.title: Webinar - Commencing and ceasing a pension in an SMSF
item.dc:creator: superguardian
item.content:encoded:
In June 2024 the ATO finalised their update of their 2013 Taxation Ruling 'TR 2013/5 Income tax: when a superannuation income stream commences and ceases'.  This update primarily refreshed the ruling to accommodate the 2017 superannuation reform measures, however, it also introduced new considerations specifically around when a pension ceases. In this webinar, Tim Miller from Smarter SMSF will review TR 2013/5 and highlight the key considerations for when a pension commences and ceases within an SMSF in light of the introduction of the transfer balance cap in 2017.  Particular focus will be given to the ramifications of an SMSF not paying the minimum pension and what that can mean for a fund's pension planning.  Learning Outcomes At the end of this webinar, participants will have an appreciation for: 
  • when a pension commences in an SMSF
  • the various circumstances that can lead to a pension ceasing within an SMSF
  • the impact of failing the pension standards i.e. not paying the minimum
  • how to deal with minimum pension shortfalls

Presenter: Tim Miller

Cost: Free

Date: Wednesday, 13th November

Time: 12:30 pm - 1:30pm AEST

Register Now

Tim Miller

Tim Miller is the SMSF Technical and Education Manager at Smarter SMSF and is one of Australia’s leading SMSF educators and presenters with over 25 years’ experience in the Superannuation industry.  His experience has been gained through providing legislative and compliance support to thousands of Trustees, Accountants and Financial Planners since joining the SMSF sector in 1999.  In addition to servicing the needs of SMSF clients, his practical and personable approach to compliance has made him a much sought after presenter.

Tim regularly presents at the major SMSF conferences and events, contributes to various trade and general publications, he is a Fellow SMSF Association Specialist and was awarded the CEO Award at the 2023 SMSF Association National Conference for services to the SMSF sector.
